```{r meta-data, include = FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(pwr)
```{r instructions, include = FALSE}
# HOW TO USE THIS TEMPLATE!
# 1. Save a copy, and change the name to PowerAnalysis_PAPERID_COLLAB_RRID (e.g. "PowerAnalysis_aYyR_Gooty_23w12.Rmd")
# 2. Fill in the variables that identify this project (if you are using a version given to you by COS staff, some may already be filled in)
# 3. Fill in all available values for the single focal inferential test, *exactly as reported in the paper*

# 4. Fill in values for the single inferential test used to conduct the power analysis. If this is the same test as reported, include just those values that are actually used to calculate the sample size.
# 5. Conduct the power analysis here!
# 6. Make sure to set the sample size variables to the result of the power analysis
# 7. If you calculated any new variables (e.g. an alternative effect size measure), add them as variable values *after* the power analysis.
# 8. Knit the file and check the report and the TSV files look like they should! You can delete this instructions block.
```{r report-vars, include = FALSE}
# Add variables to identify files/make report readable
paper_id = NA # ex. '5Xaw' From Asana ticket/pdf_filename
collab_name = NA #ex. 'Smith' From Asana ticket or minting data
rr_id = NA #rr_attempt id string, From Asana ticket or minting data
claim_id = NA #TBD but needed for data set, expect to backfill later but leave NA for now
pdf_filename = NA # 'Crossley_OrgBehavior_2009_5Xaw'
first_author = NA # 'Crossley' derive from pdf filename
pub_year = NA     #'2009'      derive from pdf filename
coded_claim4 = '' #From tagtog/CE data, e.g. 'Malice related indirectly to revenge (b = .19, p < .05)'
original_samplesize_calculation_contributor = '' #"Olivia Miske"
original_analysis_type_cos = NA # e.g. 'Between-subjects ANOVA', 'Multisample Structural Equation Model (MSEM)'
original_materials_link = NA # "https://osf.io/rg5bh/?view_only=31f90788606c44bd2f99ad"
rr_poweranalysis_link = NA # "http://www.osf.io/rg5ve"
rr_type = NA #FACTOR: 'Replication'|'Generalizability Study'
# Sample Size Calculation for a `r rr_type` of `r first_author` et al. (`r pub_year`)
The following is the brief description of the statistical analysis for the focal inferential test in SCORE and the results of that test. See the paper itself for additional background.
* `r coded_claim4`
## Inferential test as reported - original paper
This template is used for cases where only **a single inferential test** from **a single claim_4** in the paper is being used as the basis of the power analysis of **a replication**.
The inferential test, exactly as it is reported in the original paper, is below:
```{r original_reported}
#NOTE: For factor variables like the statistic and effect size types, you should use one of the existing options if appropriate, and contact Melissa if you have a new entry for the list.
original_statistic_fulltext_reported = NA #All available statistical information expressed as 1 or more equations, e.g. "b = .19, p < .05" or "F(1,23) = 2.34, p = 0.023, Cohen's d= 0.87"
